pattern: Rewriting the pair condition as nums[i] minus i lets you count the easy good pairs by equal key, then subtract from the total.
hints: Rearrange j - i != nums[j] - nums[i] so each index contributes a single fixed quantity.
Two indices form a good pair exactly when nums[i] - i equals nums[j] - j, so group indices by that difference.
Count good pairs as the sum over each key of choose(count, 2), then subtract from n*(n-1)/2 to get the bad ones.

edge_inputs.description: two equal elements form one bad pair
mixed keys produce both good and bad pairs
all identical values, every pair is bad except none align

stress.description: large random array exposing any O(n^2) pair enumeration
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def countBadPairs(self, nums):
        from collections import defaultdict
        n = len(nums)
        cnt = defaultdict(int)
        good = 0
        for i, v in enumerate(nums):
            key = v - i
            good += cnt[key]
            cnt[key] += 1
        return n * (n - 1) // 2 - good
